member.summary: Case change import excel command parameters

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.Excel.CaseChangeImport
member.summary: Imports case change data from an Excel workbook into an model.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.Excel.CaseChangeImport.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The HTTP client for backend API access.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.Excel.CaseChangeImport.ReadCaseChangesAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Reads case changes from an Excel file and returns them as an exchange model.
member.param: The path to the Excel file.
member.returns: The exchange model containing the imported case changes.
member.exception: Thrown when the file is missing, tenant or user is unknown, or data is invalid.

member.param: The case and case field namespace

member.summary: Reads case fields from the sheet and assigns them to the corresponding cases.
member.param: The previously imported cases to populate with fields.
member.exception: Thrown when required columns are missing, referenced cases are unknown, names are empty, or duplicates are found.

member.summary: Reads a regulation including cases, collectors, wage types, reports, lookups, and scripts from an Excel file.
member.param: The path to the Excel file.
member.returns: The exchange model containing the imported regulation.
member.exception: Thrown when the file is missing, tenant is undefined, or regulation data is invalid.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.HttpCommands.HttpCommandBase`1
member.summary:
Base class for http commands. Supports URL placeholders resolved against the backend at runtime. Placeholders are resolved left-to-right, so {tenant:X} must appear before any tenant-scoped placeholder in the URL.
Supported placeholders: {tenant:Identifier} → numeric tenant id {user:Identifier} → numeric user id (requires tenant placeholder before) {division:Name} → numeric division id (requires tenant placeholder before) {employee:Identifier} → numeric employee id (requires tenant placeholder before) {regulation:Name} → numeric regulation id (requires tenant placeholder before) {payroll:Name} → numeric payroll id (requires tenant placeholder before) {payrun:Name} → numeric payrun id (requires tenant placeholder before) {payrunJob:Name} → numeric payrun job id (requires tenant placeholder before)

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.HttpCommands.HttpCommandBase`1.ResolveUrlAsync(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,System.String)
member.summary: Resolve {type:value} placeholders in a URL against the backend. Placeholders are resolved left-to-right — {tenant:X} must precede all tenant-scoped placeholders.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.HttpCommands.HttpGetParameters.FileName
member.summary: Output file name (optional) — when provided, response body is written to this file

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestCleanupCommand
member.summary: Delete load test employees (identified by filter pattern in identifier)

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestCleanupParameters.FilterPattern
member.summary: Employee identifier filter pattern (substring match, e.g. "-C" for load test copies)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand
member.summary: Generate scaled exchange files for payrun load testing

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.ExtractEmployeeTemplates(PayrollEngine.Client.Model.ExchangeTenant,System.Collections.Generic.List{PayrollEngine.Client.Model.CaseChangeSetup})
member.summary: Extract employee templates from exchange data. Handles two cases: - Template has Employees section → use EmployeeSet objects - Template has NO Employees section → extract identifiers from case changes Division is resolved from existing employee or from case change DivisionName.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.CreateEmployeeFromIdentifier(System.String)
member.summary: Create an EmployeeSet from an identifier. Parses LastName and FirstName from the identifier parts.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.GenerateScaledEmployees(System.Collections.Generic.List{PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.EmployeeTemplate},System.Int32)
member.summary: Generate N employees by multiplying templates

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.ExtractInvocations(PayrollEngine.Client.Model.ExchangeTenant,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extract unique invocations from template, deduplicated by PeriodStart. Removes EmployeeIdentifiers so all employees are included.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateCommand.ScaledEmployee
member.summary: Scaled employee with reference to original template

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestGenerateParameters
member.summary: Load test exchange file generator parameters

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesCommand
member.summary: Bulk-import case changes for load testing via AddCasesBulkAsync

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesParameters)
member.summary: Bulk-import case changes for load testing

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesCommand.ResolveCaseFiles(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve case files from a path (directory or single file)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesParameters
member.summary: Load test case bulk setup parameters

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesParameters.CasesPath
member.summary: Directory containing Setup-Cases-*.json files, or a single file path

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupCasesParameters.BatchSize
member.summary: HTTP batch size for bulk API calls (default: 500)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.LoadTestSetupEmployeesCommand
member.summary: Setup employees for load testing via bulk API

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestCommand
member.summary: Execute payrun load test with timing metrics

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestCommand.WaitForJobCompletion(PayrollEngine.Client.Service.Api.PayrunJobService,PayrollEngine.Client.Service.TenantServiceContext,System.Int32,PayrollEngine.Client.Command.ICommandConsole)
member.summary: Poll job until processing is complete (JobEnd is set). Uses adaptive polling: starts fast (100ms) for small jobs, then backs off to 1s for long-running jobs.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestCommand.TakeSystemSnapshot
member.summary: Take a point-in-time system resource snapshot

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestCommand.GetWindowsAvailRamGb
member.summary: P/Invoke: available physical RAM in GB via GlobalMemoryStatusEx

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestCommand.CreateInvocation(PayrollEngine.Client.Model.PayrunJobInvocation,System.String)
member.summary: Create a fresh invocation with unique name for each run

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestExcelWriter
member.summary: Writes payrun load test results to an Excel workbook

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestMarkdownWriter
member.summary: Writes payrun load test results to a Markdown report

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestMarkdownWriter.GetProcessorName
member.summary: Returns the CPU model name from the Windows Registry, or architecture on other platforms

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestParameters.ExcelReport
member.summary: Output Excel path for results (optional, enables Excel report)

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestParameters.MarkdownReport
member.summary: Output Markdown path for results (optional, enables Markdown report)

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestParameters.ParallelSetting
member.summary: Backend MaxParallelEmployees value for documentation in the Excel report

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestParameters.HasExcelReport
member.summary: True if an Excel report should be written

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestParameters.HasMarkdownReport
member.summary: True if a Markdown report should be written

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.PayrunLoadTestResult.EmployeeCount
member.summary: Number of employees in this run

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.SystemSnapshot.DiskFreeGb
member.summary: Disk free space in GB (drive of working directory)

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.LoadTestCommands.SystemSnapshot.RamAvailableGb
member.summary: Available physical RAM in GB (Windows only, 0 on other platforms)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollConvertCommand
member.summary: Convert payroll JSON from/to YAML command

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollConvertCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollConvertParameters)
member.summary: Convert a JSON or YAML file

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollConvertCommand.ConvertFileAsync(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,System.String,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.SchemaType)
member.summary: Convert a file between json and yaml

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollExportCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollExportParameters)
member.summary: Export a tenant to a JSON file. Default the file name is the tenant identifier including a timestamp

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollImportCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollImportParameters)
member.summary: Import a tenant from a JSON file

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollMergeCommand
member.summary: Merge multiple payroll files into one file

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollMergeCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrollCommands.PayrollMergeParameters)
member.summary: Convert a JSON or YAML file

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeePreviewTestCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeePreviewTestParameters)
member.summary: Test employee payrun via preview specified in a payroll exchange JSON file The test sequence is: 1. Load test data from exchange file (payrun job invocations and expected results) 2. Execute payrun job preview for each invocation 3. Compare the preview results with the expected results

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeePreviewTestParameters
member.summary: Payrun employee preview test command parameters

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeePreviewTestParameters.ActualOutputFile
member.summary: Optional output file path for actual payroll results as JSON. When set, the actual WageType and Collector results are written to this file in payrollResults format — ready for direct use as expected values in .et.json files.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeeTestCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeeTestParameters)
member.summary: Test employee payrun specified in a payroll exchange JSON file The test sequence is: 1. Import employee case changes, payrun jobs and expected results (payroll, payrun job and employee must be present) 2. Create test employee, copy of existing employee 3. Setup test employee on jobs and results 4. Execute payrun jobs 5. Compare the file results with the calculated employee results (results must be present)

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeeTestCommand.InjectPayrollResultPlaceholders(PayrollEngine.Client.Model.Exchange)
member.summary: Injects minimal payrollResults placeholders into the exchange so that TestPayrunJobAsync runs fully and populates ActualResult on each test result. Each invocation gets one placeholder with empty wageTypeResults and collectorResults. The employee identifier is taken from the first case change in the payroll.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeeTestCommand.WriteActualResultsAsync(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{PayrollEngine.Client.Model.Tenant,System.Collections.Generic.List{PayrollEngine.Client.Test.Payrun.PayrollTestResult}},System.String)
member.summary: Serializes actual WageType and Collector results from all test results to a JSON file. Output format matches the payrollResults block in .et.json — ready for direct use as expected values without manual editing. Employee identifier is reverse-mapped from the test clone ("X Test N" → "X").

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunEmployeeTestParameters.ActualOutputFile
member.summary: Optional output file path for actual payroll results as JSON. When set, the actual WageType and Collector results are written to this file in payrollResults format — ready for direct use as expected values in .et.json files. The test run proceeds normally; this is a side-effect only.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunTestCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunTestParameters)
member.summary: Test a payrun specified in a payroll exchange JSON file The test sequence is: 1. Read test payroll 2. Create test tenant (existing tenant will be deleted) 3. Execute payrun job (payrun job must be present) 4. Compare the file results with the calculated payrun results (results must be present) 5. Remove test payroll

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.PayrunCommands.PayrunTestCommandBase.RunProgressAsync(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.ICommandConsole,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Show a waiting indicator every 10 seconds until cancelled

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.ProgramExitCode.GenericError
member.summary: Generic application error (from client core command)

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.ProgramExitCode.CommandFile
member.summary: Command file error (from client core command)

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.ProgramExitCode.InvalidInput
member.summary: Invalid input (e.g. missing or invalid source files)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageCommand
member.summary: InstallRegulationPackage command — installs a regulation NuGet package into a PE backend tenant. The package source can be a local file path, a file wildcard, or an HTTP(S) URL.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageParameters)
member.summary: Install a regulation package into the backend

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageCommand.ResolvePackageSource(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.ICommandConsole,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the resolved package source: - HTTP(S) URLs are passed through unchanged - Existing local paths are returned as-is - Wildcards are resolved to a single match

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageParameters.PackageFile
member.summary: Path to the .nupkg file to install

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageParameters.Tenant
member.summary: Target tenant identifier — the tenant into which the regulation is imported

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageParameters.InstallMode
member.summary: Install mode: New (default) fails if regulation already exists; Overwrite replaces

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.InstallRegulationPackageParameters.DryRun
member.summary: Dry-run mode: Execute (default) performs the import; DryRun validates without changes

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextImport.ImportAsync
member.summary: Import regulation lookups from text files

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextImport.ImportFileAsync(System.IO.FileInfo)
member.summary: Import regulation lookup from text file

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextImportCommand
member.summary: Import lookup from raw text file, converting a file lines into lookup values.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextMap
member.summary: Mapping from text line to lookup value

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextValueMap
member.summary: Mapping from text line part to lookup key/value part

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.LookupTextValueMap.Start
member.summary: Value start index in text line (0..n)

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationDryRun.DryRun
member.summary: Validate and report what would be imported without making any changes

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationExcelImportCommand.Execute(PayrollEngine.Client.Command.CommandContext,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationExcelImportParameters)
member.summary: Import a tenant from an Excel file

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationInstallMode.New
member.summary: Fail if the regulation already exists in the target tenant (default)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageInstaller
member.summary: Installs a regulation NuGet package (.nupkg) into a PE backend tenant. The package source can be a local file path, a file wildcard, or an HTTP(S) URL. The package is opened as a ZIP archive; the manifest and import files are read from the regulation/ folder within the archive.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageInstaller.InstallAsync(System.String,System.String,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationInstallMode,PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationDryRun)
member.summary: Installs the regulation package into the specified tenant.
member.param: Local file path, file wildcard, or HTTP(S) URL to the .nupkg
DryRun validates without importing; Execute performs the import

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageInstaller.ResolveLocalFileAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the path to a local .nupkg file. If is an HTTP(S) URL the package is downloaded to a temp file. The caller is responsible for deleting it when done.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageManifest
member.summary: Regulation package manifest — embedded as regulation-package.json in the NuGet package. Defines install metadata and the ordered list of files to import.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageManifest.RegulationName
member.summary: PE regulation name as registered in the backend (e.g. Acme.Regulation.Country)

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageManifest.BaseRegulations
member.summary: PE regulation names this package depends on. Each must already be installed in the target tenant before this package can be installed.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.RegulationCommands.RegulationPackageManifest.InstallFiles
T:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.ReportCommands.ReportBuildCommand
member.summary:
Report build command — executes a report and exports the resulting DataSet as a schema document for use during report template design.
Without TemplateFile: generates a new schema document from the DataSet. With TemplateFile (CI mode): updates the schema section of the existing template, preserving all design elements. Useful when regulation changes add or remove tables.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.ReportCommands.ReportBuildParameters.TemplateFile
member.summary: Existing FastReport template file (.frx). When provided, the command updates the Dictionary section of this template and writes the result to the target file (CI mode). When omitted, a new skeleton .frx is generated.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.SchemaType.Auto
member.summary: Automatic schema base on file extension

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.PayrollConsole.Commands.Script.Publisher.PublishFunction(PayrollEngine.Client.Scripting.FunctionAttribute,PayrollEngine.Client.Scripting.ScriptAttribute,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MethodDeclarationSyntax)
member.summary: Publishes function script by line number
